title: Crunchy Citrusy Filipino Muscovado Sugar Leche Flan
description: A Filipino dessert featuring muscovado sugar, crunchy citrusy topping, and a creamy leche flan base, perfect for a date night, this dish is gluten-free friendly and has a unique texture from the deep-fry tod crunch, the citrusy flavor is achieved through the use of citrus zest and juice in the recipe.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, combine muscovado sugar, orange zest, and lemon zest, and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar has melted and the mixture is caramelized.
In a large bowl, whisk together eggs, sweetened condensed milk, evaporated milk, and vanilla extract.
Strain the egg mixture into a clean bowl to remove any air bubbles.
Pour the egg mixture into 6 1/2 cup ramekins or custard cups.
Place the ramekins in a large baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
Bake for 45 minutes, or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly.
Remove the ramekins from the water bath and let them cool to room temperature.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or overnight.
Just before serving, sprinkle the crushed tod crunch over the top of each leche flan.
